title: 5/10/19 Swam 7,520 meters, food shopped & cleaned house

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-log. Hopefully I can get through this video before it getting shut out.
I get troll calls all day long now.
Hasn't been happening for very long, ever since I started doing videos again.
So, yesterday I went to the Costco and got food, $180 worth of food.
And then, so I've got enough food for the rest of the month. It's awesome.
I don't have to spend a single more penny.
Um, and, uh, got up kind of late, like 11.30 yesterday.
And, uh, by the, and then I had to do all this kind of house cleaning, you know, put my food away.
And then, uh, clean my fish tank, um, sweep, um, some other things that I haven't done for a while.
So, by the time I was able to go swimming, that was the thing I did yesterday.
It was four o'clock, um, and I swam until it got dark and then came in.
And, uh, I wanted to do, um, um, uh, 10 kilometers, but I made it 7,620 before it got dark and I kind of lost my motivation, got cold, got tired.
And then I got home and I realized how tired I actually was.
Um, and I had, uh, uh, lasagna.
So, the only thing I ate yesterday was, uh, um, a turkey sandwich, uh, a bottle of, um, a kai juice and one thing of lasagna.
So, uh, my appetite has not kicked in right now.
It's 45 degrees and it says it's, it's raining and it looks like it's out and it looks like it's raining about to rain.
It's super cool.
So, I don't want to go outside.
I need as much, um, motivation as I can get and freezing cold weather ain't going to motivate me.
So, I'm going to, um, um, today I'm thinking I'm going to, um, I still have to finish my, uh, my book about, uh, popular history of Ireland and then I can be done.
So, I think I'm going to, um, I think I'm going to work on my website while I ride my bike.
Um, just take a nice easy day of spinning today.

Um, and, uh, because I'm, I'm connecting links to all of my castles and, uh, that's a very exciting, that's a very exciting project for me because, um, um, it's kind of like, uh, once I get these castles,
Castles linked and get them into their proper places, it'll make my timeline look, look kind of ready.
It'll take it way to the next level.
So, and it's not really that long of a process.
Um, I already have the, uh, Google Earth links ready.
KMZ Google Earth files.
I already have the, uh, Flickr file ready to link.
Um, I don't have the movie.
I set up a, um, I set up a, uh, move, a, uh, movie icon next to my castles.
And, um, so that means that I intended on making a movie of the castle.
If I had been making my timeline now, I wouldn't have done that.
It would have seemed a little bit overkill.
But now that I think about it, it actually seems like a really cool thing to do because it gives you the 360.
The movie, what I mean is a three, you just go down, you click on the Google Earth link and it takes you down maybe a hundred feet or something over the castle, a couple hundred feet over the castle.
And then you can go up and, yeah, what I do is I start looking at the castle.
I go up and then I do a 360 around the castle and, um, and you can see what the, uh, what the, um, the, uh, land and everything around it looks like.
So I think that's important, uh, you know, if you're trying to know what kind of castle, know the castle you're talking about.
So, uh, so that means I have to create those movies and they're at least a minute each.
Those, those, uh, uh, um, panoramas and there's 2,250 or something of those castles.
So that's how many minutes it is.
So it's a lot of work.
Um, I'll do the links to the castles and the, um, and the, uh, flicker links first to all of the castles that I have.
And then depending on if I'm still motivated to work on this, I'll just go ahead and build the 360s.
Uh, I don't think I can build the 360s unless I'm laying in bed though.
So it'll be kind of one of those things that I do when I'm, you know, watching move, watching movies or documentaries with my fiance.
I don't ever just watch the documentary.
I also do work.
So I could do the work and then kind of watch it with my big TV, do the work with my computer here and then watch it with my big screen TV.
So, um, and then also like, well, I'm tired and laying in bed and stuff.
So I got a pretty good sleep last night.
I feel, I still feel kind of beat.
I haven't been working out for that long.
My appetite hasn't kicked in.
Um, I think I did have a little bit of insomnia and getting going.
I didn't get to sleep until like two in the morning and then I slept in.
It's like 11 something now.
So, uh, 1150 now.
So I haven't had anything to eat.
So I'm going to pig out and then I'm going to, yeah, get on that bike and just work on the links all day long.
Um, until it gets dark, until it gets late, like 10 o'clock, who knows?
Um, and that'll be enough exercise for me.
Maybe I'll get off and do some weightlifting in between.
But, uh, I like the idea of work, making these like, it's been years since I've, uh, since I've done this, these castles.
And my timeline said, I'll feel like I have some movement on it.
And, uh, yeah, as soon as the weather's good, I can start training my ass off.
But when it's 45 degrees and snowy, I just, I'll just stay inside.
Um, I was thinking about doing 20 kilometer time trials on my bike, but I'm too motivated to work on my timeline.
You know, I have to keep moving forward on my timeline a little bit.
But, all right, later, talk to you tomorrow.
